task_completion.description: The target image is opened in the GIMP application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GIMP application window with a photograph loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Shadows-Highlights tool dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Shadows-Highlights' dialog box floating over the GIMP interface (typically accessed via the 'Colors' menu).

task_completion.description: The Shadows slider is adjusted to brighten the dark areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Shadows-Highlights' dialog box. The slider labeled 'Shadows' should be moved to the right (a positive value) compared to its default zero position.

task_completion.description: The tonal adjustments are applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the disappearance of the 'Shadows-Highlights' dialog box after adjustments were made, indicating the user clicked 'OK' to apply the effect to the image canvas.

task_completion.description: The final image shows noticeably brighter shadows and improved tonal balance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Compare the final state of the image in the canvas to the initial state. The dark, shadowed areas of the photograph should appear noticeably brighter and reveal more detail, while the overall image retains a good tonal balance (highlights are not overly blown out).

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent interacted with GIMP's menus and dialogs (like Colors > Shadows-Highlights) rather than typing image processing commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent modified the provided image rather than opening a pre-edited file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the image modification happened progressively within the GIMP canvas through tool usage.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that no Python scripts, ImageMagick commands, or other terminal-based image processing tools were executed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP 2.10 to brighten shadows and improve tonal balance. The target image is a photo with significant shadow areas (either Unsplash photo-1506905925346... or photo-1469474968028...). The standard method is using the 'Shadows-Highlights' tool (Colors > Shadows-Highlights). The programmatic verifier checks for: 1) Shadow brightness increase >= 10%, 2) Very dark pixel reduction >= 25%, and 3) Meaningful change (>5 luminance points) in at least 10% of pixels. The VLM should visually confirm that the dark areas of the image are noticeably brightened without ruining the highlights.
